A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN YELLOW BARN STATE FOREST, EAST OF YELLOW BARN ROAD. IT THEN TRACKED EASTWARD AND STRUCK A FARM ON IRISH SETTLEMENT ROAD, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING MULTIPLE TREES. THE TORNADO THEN STRUCK THE YARD OF A HOUSE ON THE WEST SIDE OF ROUTE 38, CAUSING SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE TO THE YARD, A SHED TO BLOW OFF ITS FOUNDATION, AND A BLOW-DOWN OF A FENCE. IT THEN CROSSED ROUTE 38 JUST SOUTH OF DRYDEN, BEFORE LIFTING JUST EAST OF THE ROAD.
